behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "commits not in main...older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR".
Slot B: Boundary signal is real git ancestry/time + real PR existence checks.
Slot C: Integration with faked git/PR data can miss real git graph or PR lookup mismatches.
Final label rationale: Slot B requires real subprocess/network boundaries, and Slot C shows Integration can hide failures, so System is minimum. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is deterministic state detection with no model judgment or multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Create real branches in a disposable repo, add commits with controlled timestamps, query real PR state for those branches, and assert detector output only marks branches stale when all three conditions hold together.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning...when the current branch is stale".
Slot B: Boundary signal is module handoff: bootstrap hook consumes computed branch-state and renderer outputs warning.
Slot C: Unit-testing only formatter or only hook logic can miss broken wiring of state-to-warning context.
Final label rationale: Slot B is cross-module wiring, and Slot C shows Unit is too low, so Integration is minimum. Rejected System because behavior text does not require live repo/network; stale state can be injected into session-start flow.
behaviors.test_description: Wire session-start initializer, stale-state provider, and warning renderer in-process; inject stale current-branch state and assert emitted warning includes branch name/age/action fields expected by bootstrap output.

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state...deterministically".
Slot B: Boundary signal is a single pure function with no I/O.
Slot C: Anything higher adds infrastructure not needed to expose mapping bugs.
Final label rationale: Slot B is purely local logic, and Slot C shows no module/infra boundary is required, so Unit is minimum. Rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly scopes to a local deterministic function.
behaviors.test_description: Table-test classifier inputs (no commits, commits+PR, commits+no-PR+old) and assert exact label outputs plus deterministic repeatability over repeated calls.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "against live repo and PR state".
Slot B: Boundary signal is real command execution plus real git/PR infrastructure.
Slot C: Mocked repo or PR clients can always return idealized states and miss command/integration failures.
Final label rationale: Slot B directly requires real subprocess/network surfaces, and Slot C shows mocks can hide failures, so System is minimum. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior concerns deterministic rendering from repo/PR state, not model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run `/kaizen-wip` in a real test repo connected to real PR metadata, exercise one clean branch and one stale-no-PR branch, and assert rendered status text matches each case.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives".
Slot B: Boundary signal is detector logic consuming PR-status state and applying stale classification rules.
Slot C: Unit-testing only one predicate can miss incorrect composition between PR-state adapter output and classifier.
Final label rationale: Slot B is module interaction over deterministic data, and Slot C shows Unit can miss handoff bugs, so Integration is minimum. Rejected System because behavior text does not require "live" PR calls and can be validated with in-process wiring of realistic PR-state fixtures.
behaviors.test_description: Wire PR-state adapter output model to staleness classifier in-process; feed branches with `open` and `merged` PR statuses and assert neither is labeled `stale-no-pr` even when commit age threshold is exceeded.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change".
Slot B: Boundary signal is real git history rewriting semantics (rebase/squash) affecting ancestry checks.
Slot C: Integration with synthetic commit IDs can miss failures in actual git graph resolution after history rewrite.
Final label rationale: Slot B depends on real VCS behavior, and Slot C shows in-process fakes are insufficient, so System is minimum. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior is git-state correctness, not AI output or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create stale branch history, then rebase/squash-merge to main so hashes change; rerun detector and assert branch is no longer classified as stale-no-PR.
